editorial.headline: Durbin Homer Over Monster Sinks New York Yankees
editorial.subheadline: Boston Red Sox beats New York Yankees.
editorial.lead_angle:
Caleb Durbin watched the pitch sail over the Green Monster and the New York Yankees’ lead disappeared with it. Durbin’s two-run shot in the fifth inning ignited a four-run rally that carried the Boston Red Sox to a 6-3 victory over their rivals at Fenway Park. The blast erased a patient, early effort from New York, turning a tight divisional game on its head in a matter of minutes. Connelly Early gets the win for Boston, while Cam Schlittler was saddled with the loss for the visitors.

The fifth frame undid everything the Yankees had built. Schlittler carried a 2-0 lead into the inning, the product of single runs driven in by Paul Goldschmidt and José Caballero. But the Boston bats came alive against him. After a leadoff single, Durbin sent a high drive over the wall in left to tie the game. The rally continued with a walk and another pair of hits, plating two more runs before New York could escape the inning, their advantage gone for good.

Durbin was the engine for the Boston offense, finishing 2-for-3 with the pivotal home run and two runs batted in. While Caballero homered for New York, Durbin’s blow was the one that tilted the field. The victory gives the Red Sox a crucial edge in the heated American League East race, tightening the gap with their Bronx rivals. Aroldis Chapman slammed the door in the ninth, striking out two to secure the save and send the Fenway crowd home happy.

flags: BIG RBI GAME: Junior Caminero (6 RBI)
